tool_args.label: MTA 2025 Preliminary Budget July Financial Plan 2025–2028 Volume 1

payload.title: MTA 2025 Preliminary Budget July Financial Plan 2025 – 2028 Volume 1
payload.source_title: MTA 2025 Preliminary Budget July Financial Plan 2025 – 2028
payload.description: Volume 1 of MTA's July Financial Plan covering the 2025 Preliminary Budget, 2024 Mid-Year Forecast, and Financial Plan for 2025-2028. Includes Executive Summary, MTA Consolidated Financial Plan, Plan Adjustments, and MTA Budget Process.

warnings: source.source_title is accepted as passthrough, but prefer title. Source title label.

source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA) is the public authority responsible for public transportation in the New York metropolitan area, operating the 2025 Preliminary Budget July Financial Plan 2025-2028.

payload.title: Chair and Chief Executive Officer
payload.description: Chair and Chief Executive Officer of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority. Certified the 2025 Preliminary Budget July Financial Plan on 7.24.24.
MTA agency operating New York City subways and buses, listed as NYCT/SIR in the MTA financial plan.
MTA commuter railroad serving Long Island. Included in the MTA 2025 Preliminary Budget July Financial Plan.
MTA commuter railroad serving the northern suburbs of New York City. Included in the MTA 2025 Preliminary Budget July Financial Plan.
MTA agency operating bus service. Included in the MTA 2025 Preliminary Budget July Financial Plan.
MTA agency operating toll bridges and tunnels (Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority). Included in the MTA 2025 Preliminary Budget July Financial Plan.
MTA-operated railway on Staten Island. Listed as NYCT/SIR in the MTA financial plan.
Federal agency from which MTA expects reimbursement of direct COVID-related expenses.

payload.status: hiring planned to start in 2026, service assumed to commence in 2028
payload.description: Extension of Metro-North Railroad's New Haven Line to reach Penn Station with four new accessible stations in the Bronx. Hiring planned to start in 2026, a one-year delay from the February Plan, with service assumed to commence in 2028.
Fare/Toll Increase, March 2025 (4% Yield) - assumed for implementation in March 2025, projected to generate $301 million in 2026, the first full year after implementation
Fare/Toll Increase, March 2027 (4% Yield) - assumed for implementation in March 2027, projected to generate $320 million in 2028, the first full year after implementation
Certification of the 2025 Preliminary Budget July Financial Plan by Chair and CEO Janno Lieber on 7.24.24
Net Cash Surplus/(Deficit) for 2027 after below-the-line adjustments
Net Cash Surplus/(Deficit) for 2028 after below-the-line adjustments
Farebox Revenue in the 2025 Preliminary Budget
Total Operating Revenues in the 2025 Preliminary Budget
Total Expenses with Debt Service in the 2025 Preliminary Budget
Total savings from operating efficiencies initiatives identified and incorporated into Agency baseline financial plans through 2027
Subway and Bus farebox revenue projected to be lower by $811 million through 2027 compared to the February Plan
Real estate transaction tax revenues projected to be lower by $790 million through 2027 compared to the February Plan
Consolidated farebox and toll revenue increase from the March 2025 fare and toll increase in 2025
Net increase to MTA from the March 2025 fare and toll increase in 2025, after subsidy impacts
Consolidated farebox and toll revenue increase from the March 2027 fare and toll increase in 2027

payload.raw_value_text: annual $200 million Farebox Revenue Loss Provision
payload.description: Annual $200 million Farebox Revenue Loss Provision included below-the-line

payload.raw_value_text: lower by $30 million through the remainder of 2024
payload.description: Estimated farebox revenue loss from CBDTP pause through the remainder of 2024 as anticipated shifts from vehicles to mass transit do not take place

payload.raw_value_text: $250 million in 2024, $130 million in 2025 and $220 million in 2026
payload.description: Total FEMA COVID-19 reimbursement expected: $250 million in 2024, $130 million in 2025, $220 million in 2026
Unidentified savings targets remaining below-the-line for the 2025 to 2028 period
NYCT/SIR expenses in the 2025 Preliminary Budget
Dedicated Taxes revenue in the 2025 Preliminary Budget
Forecasted Debt Service for 2025

payload.claim_text: The July Plan remains balanced through 2026 with deficits of $428 million in 2027 and $469 million in 2028
payload.statement: The July Plan presents a balanced budget through 2026, with deficits of $428 million in 2027 and $469 million in 2028.

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

payload.claim_text: The Plan does not yet include impacts to operating expenses and debt service from the pause in congestion pricing under the assumption that either the congestion pricing pause will be lifted, or a replacement revenue stream will be provided in a timely manner.
payload.statement: The Plan does not yet include impacts from the pause in congestion pricing.

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

payload.claim_text: Commuter Railroad farebox revenue favorable by $114 million through 2027 and B&T toll revenue favorable by $209 million through 2027
payload.statement: Projections for Commuter Railroad farebox revenue and toll revenue at B&T facilities are favorable to the February Plan.

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

payload.gap_text: Farebox revenue for 2025 through 2028 has not yet been lowered, awaiting an outcome to the congestion pricing pause.
payload.missing_information: Farebox revenue adjustments for 2025-2028 from the congestion pricing pause have not been included pending clarity on the duration of the pause.

payload.description: Penn Station Access is an extension of Metro-North Railroad's New Haven Line
The MTA 2025 Preliminary Budget July Financial Plan was published by the Metropolitan Transportation Authority
